evals.query: I'm building a Kora HTTP API. Create a JSON request DTO UserRequest with name and email, and a UserResponse with id, name, email, createdAt.
expected_behavior: Annotates each DTO type with @Json from ru.tinkoff.kora.json.common.annotation
Uses Java records (or Kotlin data classes) for the DTOs
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors" and implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module" (Kotlin: ksp symbol-processors)
Notes that the @KoraApp interface must extend JsonModule
Does not use foreign @JsonProperty or field injection; uses constructor injection

evals.query: Add a Kora @HttpController UserController with a POST /users that takes a JSON body and a GET /users/{id} that returns JSON.
expected_behavior: Marks the request parameter with @Json and the route method with @Json
Uses @HttpRoute(method = HttpMethod.POST/GET, path = ...) and @Path for the id
Annotates the controller with @HttpController and @Component, injecting the service via the constructor
Does not use @RestController, @RequestBody, @GetMapping or @PathVariable

evals.query: My JSON uses snake_case keys like user_id and first_name, and some fields are optional. How do I map this in a Kora DTO?
expected_behavior: Uses @JsonField("user_id") to rename fields (Kotlin: @field:JsonField)
Marks optional fields with @Nullable (Kotlin: nullable type T?)
Explains that all fields are required by default
Does not introduce fasterxml @JsonProperty

evals.query: Create a polymorphic JSON result PaymentResult with a discriminator field type and two cases PaymentSuccess (SUCCESS) and PaymentError (ERROR).
expected_behavior: Defines a sealed interface annotated with @Json and @JsonDiscriminatorField("type")
Annotates each subtype with @JsonDiscriminatorValue matching the JSON value
Includes the discriminator field as a component on each subtype and uses permits/implements
Does not use Jackson @JsonTypeInfo/@JsonSubTypes

evals.query: I have a PATCH /users/{id} endpoint that must distinguish a field omitted from the body from a field explicitly set to null. How do I model the request DTO in Kora?
expected_behavior: Uses JsonNullable<T> for the patchable fields
Reads state with isDefined() and value() (and isNull() when needed), not Optional-style isPresent()/get()
Explains the three states: defined non-null, defined null, undefined (omitted)
Does not invent isAbsent()/isPresent() methods on JsonNullable

evals.query: I need to serialize LocalDate as dd.MM.yyyy across my Kora app. Register a custom JSON mapper.
expected_behavior: Provides a JsonReader<LocalDate> and JsonWriter<LocalDate> factory in a @Module (@DefaultComponent) or directly in @KoraApp
Handles null in the writer (writeNull) and caches the DateTimeFormatter
Explains the factory is picked up for any @Json DTO containing LocalDate
Does not rely on framework converters or Config.getString which does not exist in Kora

evals.query: Create an OrderStatus enum that serializes to lowercase strings like pending and shipped in a Kora @Json DTO.
expected_behavior: Annotates the enum with @Json
Overrides toString() to return the lowercase value and provides a fromString lookup for reading
Explains that by default enums serialize via toString()
Does not use fasterxml enum annotations

evals.query: My team has an existing Jackson ObjectMapper config. How do I make a Kora app use Jackson instead of the generated mappers?
expected_behavior: Extends JacksonModule on the @KoraApp interface
Adds annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:json-annotation-processor" and implementation "ru.tinkoff.kora:jackson-module"
Provides a factory returning a custom ObjectMapper in the graph to override the default
Does not claim a non-existent @ExceptionHandler or JacksonHttpServerRequestMapper API
